channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Warframe Reveals Jade Shadows: Constellations With Intense New Animated Short & First Details
item.content:encoded:
Warframe’s mysterious “Unannounced 2026 update” is Jade Shadows: Constellations, a surprise sequel to 2024’s Jade Shadows. Digital Extremes revealed the update in the latest Devstream, along with an animated short made in collaboration with THE LINE and the metal band ERRA.

The new animated short reveals two rivals from two future timelines, clashing to the sounds of “Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven” by ERRA. There are two versions to watch, one centering each of the two characters, Orion and Sirius. These two rivals turn out to be (spoiler!) the Stalker’s children. Jade Shadows: Constellations puts you in control of the Stalker once more, this time as a father. You’ll have either Sirius or Orion based on choices you made in Jade Shadows, and watch the potential futures collide.

The new Narrative Chapter will expand greatly on this when it’s out in June. Also coming in the update are new Protoframes. Vena and Ryoku are the Protoframe versions of Garuda and Ash. Each will have been caring for one of the brothers and will play a big role in the events and conflict.

The Uranus-Proxima region of the Railjack Star Chart is going to open, revealing two new mission nodes connected to Vena and Ryoku. 

We’ll also see a Nidus retouch, with updates including boosted health and ability improvements. Also on the way? Dante Tytonis, the deluxe incarnation of Dante, in addition to a new skin for his Tome.

Returning much sooner is the Operation: Belly of the Beast, which has you help the Stalker defend the memory of Jade from Parvoa Granum. This will be back from May 4th to June 1st and will have new and returning rewards,  including some that you earned by playing with clanmates.

Expect a new Devstream in May for lots more on Jade Shadows: Constellations and more.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/warframe-reveals-jade-shadows-constellations-with-intense-new-animated-short-and-first-details-2000137899
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy XIV's Next Expansion, Evercold, Takes Players To The Fourth - Here Are All The Details From Today's Keynote
item.content:encoded:
During Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest in Anaheim, California, Square Enix officially revealed Final Fantasy XIV - Evercold, the next expansion launching January 2027 for all platforms. The Japanese title roughly translates to “Wanderer (or Explorer) of the Silver.” There was a metric ton of details shared on both Evercold and some massive foundation-changing updates for FFXIV at large. We’ll cover the big highlights here.

Evercold Details

This expansion moves FFXIV into what Square Enix says is the next major story arc for the MMORPG, called the “Godless Realms Saga.” The teaser trailer is framed as a bedtime story for an Au’ra child, taking place on a frozen planet where the Warrior of Light, Alphinaud, and Estinien come through a portal – which director Naoki Yoshida confirmed is the world of the Fourth Reflection. This reflection is slowly being ravaged by ever-spreading ice.

Yoshida kept story details vague, but did say that the upcoming Patch 7.5 will directly set up Evercold. During the keynote Yoshida shared a number of screenshots for new areas from the expansion, and while we didn’t get any names or major details, there’s a clear sort of high-fantasty, or almost Norse Mythology inspiration. Equally interesting is the fact that despite the expansion being called Evercold, many of the environments shown off were heavily mechanical and filled with lava and fire – suggesting something more is happening. 

While showing off some of Evercold’s town area concept art, Yoshida noted the style is “fantastical,” and that’s a clear indication of the art direction this expansion is taking. There were heavily gilded residential areas filled with spires, multicolored flower gardens, and seaside fishing shacks. 

The keynote also shared a look at a delightful new race introduced with Evercold, squat little cat-people that look a lot like dwarves. Unfortunately, we didn’t learn any more about them quite yet. 

Evercold will feature all of the normal expansion additions, including raising the level cap to 110, new dungeons, a new 8-player raid, new gear and recipes, and more.

There will be a new Alliance Raid called “Ghosts of Desire,” themed around Neon Genesis Evangelion, in collaboration for the series’ 30th anniversary. Square Enix will be developing the raid content, while the producer of Evangelion, Khara, will be supervising. 

Game System Changes

After a brief look at narrative and visual elements, Yoshida covered some of the game design updates coming with 8.0. Yoshida stressed that the last 13 years of FFXIV have allowed the game to grow to this point, but the development team wants to go a direction with Evercold – an emphasis on the game “growing” and “evolving.”

Yoshida emphasized this by showing off a completely new and rebooted Adventurer Activity screen. The game will move from Daily-based systems to Weekly-based systems, specifically for the bonuses attached to tomestones. This is an effort to combat the “weariness” that some players have with the daily systems, hoping to better fit the playstyles and lifestyles of FFXIV players. There will now be weekly missions that reward Activity Points. As you earn Activity Points you’ll raise your Activity Level, which gives you gear and other rewards. Some missions can only be completed once, while others are repeatable. Yoshida notes that the system is not fixed in any way, and will likely be adjusted over time. 

There are changes for item level progression as well, with new ways to raise item level. However, we won’t learn those details until Fan Fest Tokyo. 

The next addition is Seasons, but Yoshida stressed this does not mean extra fees or the typical idea of a Season Pass. FFXIV’s Seasons equals 2 patches (Even+Odd), for example 8.0 and 8.1 will be one “Season.” This will hopefully enable the development team to shorten the time between patches by sort of bundling them together. 

Tne of the focal points of Evercold’s game design is leaning into making each and every player’s character different. This starts with an update to the Armoury System, something that has barely been changed in over a decade. In 8.0 jobs will still exist and be leveled separately, but gear will reflect your highest item level in your Armoury, irrespective of job. This will allow more freedom in how players can approach content and playing as jobs, such as being able to take a job into a Savage Raid that you may not have the right gear for. More details on this system will be revealed in the future. 

The other major addition is Character Action Skins, letting you customize your characters with specific actions – giving you more freedom to set apart your avatar. More details on these skins will be revealed at Fan Fest Berlin. 

Character creation options will be improved and broadened, increasing the options you have for customizing your avatar. These include a color picker for hair, eyes, and skin tone, face paint layering, and improved customization of eyes and lips. You’ll even be able to toggle on and off certain pieces of gear, allowing for more outfit variety. A few comparison screenshots were shown off. All of the options shown off at Anaheim are planned for the 8.0 release, but additional updates will come later on. 

The next topic covered was what Square Enix is calling “Improving Public Fields.” To do this, more dynamic events and content will be added to field areas. Alongside this, the development team is looking to improve how quests are played – quality of life features that will “ease the burden” of questing. Again, this is a topic that will be explored more at Fan Fest Berlin. 

Battle content will be enhanced in three key ways

  • Adding a third difficulty level for 8-person raids (More details at Berlin)
  • Adjusting progression routes for 24-persona raids (More details at Japan)
  • All-new types of battle content

The final Game Design update is same-region player matching, meaning player matching will include all worlds across an entire region – a much-requested fan feature. This includes Party Finder. These updates will be coming with the Patch 7.5x series, with first tests happening in Japan before expanding to the US, EU, and China. 

Job Changes

In terms of jobs, Yoshida confirmed Evercold will have two new jobs, a Tank and Ranged DPS. Both jobs are entirely original to FFXIV, instead of based on legacy jobs from past Final Fantasy games. 

But the bigger news is a change to the job action system, as the game will now have a Reborn Mode and Evolved Mode. These two modes will only be available for the 21 main jobs, and not the limited jobs of Blue Mage and Beastmaster. 

Reborn Mode is a familiar system, essentially the job action and rotations that players have been using since A Realm Reborn. It’ll exist for all 21 current jobs. Reborn is what players know, but like always will be updated with new expansions and patches. 

Meanwhile, Evolved Mode will also be available for all 21 jobs, and emphasizes individuality with different rotations. All jobs will have new action rotations different from the Reborn Mode, with fewer inputs. Yoshida says Evolved Mode is not a “watered down” or simplified version of the game, instead focused on giving the game a modern feel. Yoshida stresses that these two modes have different actions, practically like two unique jobs. Job level and gear is shared and you can toggle between the modes at any time outside of battle. All game content will be clearable with both modes. 

Most importantly, however, all jobs introduced from 8.0 and on will only have Evolved Mode. 

PS4 Version Update

Yoshida announced that FFXIV is nearing a breaking point for support on PS4, as the game is simply becoming too large for the system. The development team estimates that things should stay the same until roughly 8.3, but eventually PS4 won’t be supported anymore

Free Trial Update

With version 8.0 the free trial will now include everything up through Shadowbringers. 

Nintendo Switch 2 Version

At the end of the keynote, Square Enix president Takashi Kiryu announced that Final Fantasy XIV is coming to Nintendo Switch 2. The version will a launch this August, with an Early Access period of one month sometime before that. 

The Nintendo Switch 2 version will require a separate subscription from other version of the game. But a Nintendo Switch Online membership will not be required. 

Find out more on Evercold at the expansion minisite.

Hayes Madsen

A connoisseur of all things RPG related, and always looks for the deeper meaning in gaming. His love of Gundam is only matched by his love of Pizza.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/final-fantasy-xivs-next-expansion-evercold-takes-players-to-the-fourth-here-are-all-the-details-from-todays-keynote-2000137898
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
